fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: North Carolina Respiratory Therapist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a respiratory therapist? Here's whether your license transfers to North Carolina - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: respiratory therapist license reciprocity north carolina, how to transfer respiratory therapist license to north carolina, respiratory therapist compact north carolina, north carolina respiratory therapist endorsement application

quick_facts.processing_time: Varies based on completeness and third-party responses
quick_facts.exam_name: National Board for Respiratory Care (NBRC) examination

license_types.title: Registered Respiratory Therapist (RRT)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a respiratory therapist in North Carolina.

steps.title: Create an Account on the NCRCB Online Application System
steps.description: Visit https://portal.ncrcb.org/ and click 'Register' to create a new user account. You will need to verify your email address before proceeding.

steps.title: Complete the Online Application
steps.description: Log into the NCRCB Online Application System, select 'Initial Licensure Application', and accurately fill out all required personal, educational, and professional information.

steps.title: Submit Required Documentation
steps.description: Upload documents such as official transcripts (sent directly from your accredited program or uploaded if permitted), verification of NBRC credentials (RRT or CRT), and any other supporting documents as requested through the NCRCB Portal.

steps.title: Pay the Application Fee
steps.description: Pay the non-refundable initial application fee of $100 through the NCRCB Online Application System using a credit card.

steps.title: Undergo Criminal Background Check
steps.description: Complete the required criminal background check procedures, including fingerprinting, as instructed by the North Carolina Respiratory Care Board.

steps.title: Monitor Application Status
steps.description: Regularly check the status of your application through the NCRCB Online Application System and respond promptly to any requests for additional information from the North Carolina Respiratory Care Board to avoid delays.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NBRC (National Board for Respiratory Care) TMC Exam.

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the NBRC. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Respiratory Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

faqs.question: How much does a North Carolina respiratory therapist license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around $100 to $200. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the NBRC (National Board for Respiratory Care) TMC Exam is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does North Carolina have reciprocity for respiratory therapists?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers respiratory therapist practice in North Carolina. To work in North Carolina, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my respiratory therapist license to North Carolina?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in North Carolina while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.processing_time: Varies based on completeness and third-party responses
reciprocity.notes: Respiratory Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

official_links.registration_steps: Visit https://portal.ncrcb.org/, click 'Register' to create a new user account. Provide your name, email, password, and security question, then verify your email address to complete registration and log in to start your application.
